user_query:
I need a quick compliance and risk check for our upcoming public events. Using the local files at input/events.csv, input/vendors.csv, input/incidents.csv, and the template at input/SafetyPlan_Template.md, please do the following and save all outputs under the output/ folder:

1) Compute these portfolio metrics across all rows in the CSVs:
   - Total events.
   - Average attendees per event (round to the nearest whole number).
   - Incident rate per 1,000 attendees across all events: total_incidents / total_attendees * 1000 (round to 1 decimal).
   - Top incident type by count (if there is a tie, choose alphabetically by incident type).
   - Permit submission rate: percentage of events with PermitsSubmitted == "Yes" (round to 1 decimal, do not include a percent sign in the numeric value unless requested below).

2) Identify non-compliant vendors based on a cross-file check between input/vendors.csv and input/events.csv using AssignedEvent -> EventName to fetch the Event Date. A vendor is non-compliant if ANY of the following is true:
   - InsuranceExpiry is before the matched Event Date (treat dates as YYYY-MM-DD).
   - ContractSigned is not "Yes".
   - BackgroundChecks is not "Yes".
Create output/non_compliant_vendors.csv with columns exactly in this order: VendorName, AssignedEvent, EventDate, NonComplianceReasons, InsuranceExpiry, ContractSigned, BackgroundChecks. For NonComplianceReasons, include one or more of these semicolon-separated tokens as applicable: insurance_expired; contract_missing; background_checks_missing.

3) Compose a concise compliance update at output/compliance_summary.md that includes:
   - An executive summary paragraph reflecting the computed metrics.
   - A bullet list showing each event and its permit status as: EventName — Permits: Yes/No.
   - The non-compliant vendor count and a brief note on primary gaps observed.
   - A short bullet list of the top three incident types with their counts (descending by count; ties broken alphabetically).

4) Edit the existing document at input/SafetyPlan_Template.md and produce output/SafetyPlan_Q3_Updated.md with these changes:
   - Replace placeholders with your computed values:
     • {{TOTAL_EVENTS}}
     • {{AVG_ATTENDEES}}
     • {{INCIDENT_RATE_PER_1000}}
     • {{TOP_INCIDENT_TYPE}}
     • {{PERMIT_COMPLIANCE_RATE}} (insert the numeric value and a literal "%" sign in the plan)
     • {{NONCOMPLIANT_VENDOR_COUNT}}
   - Replace the content between the markers <!-- RISK_HOTSPOTS_START --> and <!-- RISK_HOTSPOTS_END --> with a bulleted "Top 3 Risk Hotspots" list. Determine the hotspots by counting occurrences of the following risk categories and selecting the three highest counts (ties broken alphabetically by the exact category label):
     • Alcohol-related incidents (count of incidents with IncidentType == "Alcohol-related")
     • Events missing permits (count of events where PermitsSubmitted != "Yes")
     • Vendors with expired insurance before event date (count of vendors where InsuranceExpiry < Event Date)
     • Vendors without signed contracts (count of vendors where ContractSigned != "Yes")
     • Vendors without background checks (count of vendors where BackgroundChecks != "Yes")
     For each hotspot item, include the category label and the count in parentheses, plus one brief mitigation sentence.

Notes:
- Treat "Yes"/"No" fields case-sensitively as provided in the input.
- Use the Date from events.csv to evaluate vendor insurance expiry. All AssignedEvent names in vendors.csv match an EventName in events.csv.
- Do not alter input files; create the three outputs: output/non_compliant_vendors.csv, output/compliance_summary.md, and output/SafetyPlan_Q3_Updated.md.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
